title: Can satellite altimetry observe coastally trapped waves on sub-monthly timescales?
justification: The altimetry data used in this study come from two distinct products: ... MIOST SWOT+nadirs : an experimental product distributed by AVISO that integrates nadir altimetry with swath data from the SWOT mission.

title: Analytical Models for Multipath and Switch Leakage for the SWOT Interferometer
justification: This paper outlines some analytical multipath models that were used for instrument design, performance analysis and mitigation of the multipath signal.

title: Investigating the Performance of Four Empirical Cross-Calibration Methods for the Proposed SWOT Mission
justification: we carried out simulations as a contingency strategy (i.e., to be ready if roll is larger than anticipated) that could be used with ground-based data. We revisit the empirical calibration algorithms with additional solving methods (e.g., based on orbit sub-cycle) and more sophisticated performance assessments with spectral decompositions.

title: Reconstruction of Surface Kinematics From Sea Surface Height Using Neural Networks
venue: Journal Of Advances In Modeling Earth Systems
justification: The Surface Water and Ocean Topography (SWOT) satellite is expected to observe sea surface height (SSH) down to scales approaching ~15 km...presenting a serious challenge for estimating surface velocities from SWOT observations.

title: Recommendations for the design of in situ sampling strategies to reconstruct fine-scale ocean currents in the context of SWOT satellite mission
justification: Our goal is to optimize the design of in situ experiments aimed to reconstruct fine-scale ocean currents (similar to 20 km), such as those that will be conducted to validate the first available tranche of SWOT data.
